data.title: The trader who was 20 times long on BTC lost $11.72 million and sold his remaining $121 million position to be closed in 7 hours
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of chain data analyst Yu Jin, trader @AguilaTrades is about to admit defeat in this 20x long BTC: he has already executed all the positions to close, and he has lost $11.72 million in this long BTC.
The trader started to close positions at 4:30 in the morning, and has reduced his positions to only $121 million so far. And the remaining positions are also being closed by him through the TWAP (time-weighted average price) strategy, and according to his settings, all positions will be closed in 7 hours.
He currently has 1,157 BTC long positions waiting to be closed, worth $121 million. He has lost $11.72 million in this long BTC.

data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 104,000 USDT and is now at 103,999.9 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 4.37%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: Trader Faces Significant Losses in Bitcoin Investment
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain data analyst Yu Jin reports that trader @AguilaTrades has incurred substantial losses in his recent Bitcoin investment. The trader, who had leveraged a 20x long position on BTC, has begun liquidating his holdings, resulting in a loss of $11.72 million. Starting at 4:30 AM, he has reduced his position to $121 million. The remaining holdings are being liquidated using a TWAP (Time Weighted Average Price) strategy, which is set to complete in seven hours. Currently, he has 1,157 BTC long positions awaiting liquidation, valued at $121 million. The trader's Bitcoin investment has resulted in a loss of $11.72 million.

data.title: Coinbase Adds Sonic (S) to Coin Listing Roadmap
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Coinbase announced on the X platform that Sonic (S) will be added to the coin listing roadmap. The launch of trading of the asset depends on the support of market makers and sufficient technical infrastructure. Once these conditions are met, the launch of trading will be announced separately.

data.title: South Korean Appeals Court Upholds Kimchi Premium Traders’ Jail Sentences
data.description:
A South Korean appeals court has upheld the jail sentences of a group of crypto traders who exploited the kimchi premium to make thousands of dollars worth of profit.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/south-korean-appeals-court-upholds-kimchi-premium-traders-jail-sentences/
